title: elephas orientalis
intro: Elephas orientalis, also known as the Indian elephant apple or wild orange, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its bark and roots containing compounds that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Elephas orientalis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and act as an antiseptic. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, powder, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote digestive health, boost the immune system, and reduce inflammation.
